label: Acetyl-CoA
summary: Central metabolic precursor derived from glucose catabolism. Entry point into the mevalonate pathway for isoprenoid biosynthesis in engineered S. cerevisiae.
citation: Ro et al., 2006. Production of the antimalarial drug precursor artemisinic acid in engineered yeast. Nature 440, 940–943.

label: HMG-CoA
summary: 3-Hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA, a key intermediate in the mevalonate pathway. Overexpression of tHMGR was critical to increase flux toward artemisinin precursors.

label: Mevalonate
summary: Six-carbon precursor produced by HMG-CoA reductase. Rate-limiting step upregulated via truncated HMGR overexpression to boost isoprenoid flux.

label: FPP
summary: Farnesyl pyrophosphate — the universal C15 isoprenoid building block. Central branch point redirected from sterol synthesis toward artemisinin production.

label: Amorphadiene
summary: First committed artemisinin precursor, produced by amorphadiene synthase (ADS) from A. annua. Heterologous expression in yeast established the dedicated biosynthetic route.

label: Artemisinic Acid
summary: Direct precursor to artemisinin, produced by cytochrome P450 CYP71AV1 via three-step oxidation of amorphadiene. Accumulated extracellularly enabling simple recovery.

label: Artemisinin
summary: Sesquiterpene endoperoxide antimalarial. Final product of the engineered pathway — the most effective treatment for Plasmodium falciparum malaria, reaching 500 million patients.
citation: Ro et al., 2006. Nature 440, 940–943. WHO Essential Medicine since 2002.
